evals.prompt: Render notes/weekly.md as a self-contained HTML page.
evals.expected_output: The agent installs npm dependencies if needed, then invokes `node scripts/render.js notes/weekly.md`. The script writes the HTML to `notes/weekly.html` (default extension swap) and prints OUTPUT/SECTIONS/MERMAID lines. The agent reports the output path.
assertions: Agent runs `npm install` from the skill directory before first use (or confirms node_modules/ already exists)
Agent invokes `node scripts/render.js notes/weekly.md` (flag order may vary)
Agent does NOT pass --output unless the user asked for a non-default path
Agent does NOT hand-write HTML, CSS, or template strings
Agent reports the OUTPUT: path printed by the script back to the user

evals.prompt: Render docs/architecture.md to architecture.html with the green accent theme and a 'v3 draft' subtitle.
evals.expected_output: The agent invokes `node scripts/render.js docs/architecture.md --output architecture.html --theme green --subtitle "v3 draft"`. The script writes the file with green accent CSS variables and the subtitle text in the header.
assertions: Agent invokes `node scripts/render.js docs/architecture.md` (flag order may vary)
Agent passes `--output architecture.html`
Agent passes `--theme green`
Agent passes `--subtitle "v3 draft"` (quoted string)
Agent does NOT pass --theme with a value not in the documented list (navy/green/teal/amber/rose)

evals.prompt: The user wants a Note callout in their rendered HTML. They wrote `**Note:** Read the migration guide first.` in their markdown. Will it work?
evals.expected_output: The agent confirms yes — the renderer detects `**Note:**` (and Tip, Warning, Important, Stop) bold lead-ins automatically and wraps the paragraph in a styled callout box. The user does not need to add HTML by hand.
assertions: Agent confirms callouts are detected automatically by the renderer
Agent does NOT instruct the user to add `<div class="callout">` HTML manually
Agent does NOT suggest a different markdown syntax (e.g., GitHub admonitions); the bold lead-in is the supported pattern
Agent lists at least three valid kinds (Note, Tip, Warning, Important, Stop)

evals.prompt: The markdown has a Mermaid diagram in a ```mermaid code fence. Will it render?
evals.expected_output:
The agent confirms yes — the renderer passes Mermaid blocks through as `
...
` and includes the Mermaid CDN script automatically when at least one Mermaid block is detected. Pass `--no-mermaid` to suppress the CDN script (diagrams will fall back to plain code blocks).
assertions: Agent confirms Mermaid is supported via runtime CDN
Agent mentions that the CDN script is auto-included only when a Mermaid block is detected
Agent does NOT manually rewrite Mermaid syntax
Agent mentions `--no-mermaid` as the way to opt out

evals.prompt: Render this markdown but the output isn't styling code blocks correctly.
evals.expected_output: The agent diagnoses the renderer (highlight.js) rather than rewriting the HTML by hand. It checks the language tag on the fence, runs the script with --verbose if available, or inspects the output's <pre><code> structure. It does NOT replace the script with a hand-written renderer.
assertions: Agent investigates the script behavior (re-runs render.js, inspects output) before concluding
Agent does NOT replace the rendered HTML by hand-editing it
Agent does NOT propose using a different tool (pandoc, etc.) without first investigating

evals.prompt: Render every .md file under docs/ to HTML.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses a shell loop: `for f in docs/*.md; do node scripts/render.js "$f"; done`. Each invocation writes a sibling .html file. The agent does NOT try to pass a glob to the renderer (the script takes a single file).
assertions: Agent uses a shell loop (for, find, or xargs) over the matching files
Agent passes one file per render.js invocation
Agent does NOT pass `docs/*.md` as a single argument to render.js (the script does not glob)
Agent reports a count of rendered files when done

evals.prompt: Render this markdown for a fully offline environment — no internet allowed.
evals.expected_output: The agent passes `--no-mermaid` so the rendered HTML doesn't reference the Mermaid CDN. The agent informs the user that any Mermaid diagrams in the source will degrade to plain code blocks rather than rendered diagrams.
assertions: Agent passes `--no-mermaid`
Agent informs the user that Mermaid diagrams will not be rendered (they remain as code blocks)
Agent does NOT propose downloading Mermaid and inlining it (script does not support that today)
